Article content: {"plain_text":"I spent 300+ hours testing Cursor, Claude Code, Lovable, and Codex for lead gen. Tried building workflows in all of them. Broke things constantly. Rebuilt from scratch more times than I can count.
The stack I landed on runs across every campaign at ColdIQ. $7M ARR agency, 400+ B2B clients, 80+ active clients. One central hub underneath all of it: Claude Code orchestrating API calls, with a single prompt generating entire campaign workflows.
I'm giving away the full setup, including the Claude Code skills that make this work (you can comment under this article and I'll dm it to you). 
But first... 
Why Claude Code Over Everything Else
Claude Code is an incredibly handy way to orchestrate API calls. You don't need to understand how APIs work. You feed it your platform's API documentation and it sorts out the connection on its own. Then you describe what you want in plain English and it builds it.
This used to be a technical endeavor. You'd have to read the documentation of each technology's API, understand how to set up each individual endpoint. Now it's as simple as saying "hey Claude, can you fetch the documentation for this platform, figure out how to connect my API key, and list me all the actions I can do with it." You provide your API key and it figures out the API connection on its own.
At ColdIQ, Claude Code is the central hub for all API and MCP calls. We leverage it for everything: creating mini-tools for lead generation, data scraping and enrichment workflows, deep SEO analysis via Google Analytics MCP, entire ad campaign and outbound campaign creation, and list building through Wiza, Prospeo, FullEnrich, and other APIs.
Three years ago, one GTM engineer at ColdIQ could manage three clients. Now that number is ten. Claude Code is what made that possible.
 
The Skills That Power It
We spent three years building the GTM system behind $7M ARR and 23M+ cold emails. We open-sourced the whole thing. Grab it by commenting under this article or the post below:
 
7 master skills covering Clay, cold email, LinkedIn Ads, LinkedIn content, list building, n8n, and signal sourcing. Each one routes your request to the right sub-skill automatically. The same system running across 70+ B2B clients right now.
These skills are what give Claude Code its context. Without them, you're just prompting a coding agent. With them, you have an agent that understands your ICP, your frameworks, your scoring criteria, and your outreach methodology.
Kenny Damian on our team built an entire outbound campaign live using Claude Code and a few GTM APIs. The setup took 5 to 6 prompts and about 12 minutes. Over 1,400 people watched it.
Ivan from our automated our entire ad campaign creation process. You type a few prompts in the terminal along with a company name, and Claude Code takes over: researching the company, building a full ICP matrix, creating audience targeting for LinkedIn Ads and Meta Ads, generating ad concepts and copy, and uploading the entire campaign straight into the ad accounts. End-to-end without leaving the terminal.
The 19 APIs Worth Connecting
 
Every ColdIQ campaign touches the same stack. Here's how the APIs connect through Claude Code. 
(The first four layers are fully covered in the skills repo we open-sourced. The last three are operational tools we use internally to run the business)
Signal layer
This is where every campaign starts. Before we write a single email, we need to know who's actually worth contacting right now.
PredictLeads monitors hiring spikes, funding rounds, and tech adoption across 100M+ companies. When a target account posts three engineering roles in a week, Claude Code can call PredictLeads' API to surface that signal and feed it into the next step automatically. 
Common Room consolidates community, product, and GTM signals from GitHub, LinkedIn, G2, and more. 
Attention captures and analyzes every sales call, so we can search conversation history for competitor mentions programmatically. 
Midbound, RB2B, and Vector de-anonymize website visitors. The people already on your site are warmer than any cold list you'll ever buy.
Data layer
Signals are useless without contact data. Claude Code calls these APIs to turn company-level signals into enriched, verified contacts.
Prospeo provides 98% verified email accuracy. Person enrichment, company enrichment, domain search, and a 200M+ contact database. I can tell Claude Code "find me 10 founders in Switzerland in software, 20-50 employees, raised funding in the past three years, and get their email and phone" and it builds the list. 
FullEnrich cascades through 20+ providers until it finds verified emails and phones, hitting 80%+ find rates. It replaced three separate enrichment subscriptions for most teams we work with. 
Openmart covers 200M+ local business records. Apollo handles firmographic and technographic data at scale. 
Wiza converts LinkedIn profiles into verified contact data with 50+ data points per contact. 
Apify runs thousands of pre-built scraping actors for LinkedIn, Google Maps, conference attendee lists, and virtually any website.
Action layer
Once contacts are enriched and scored, Claude Code pushes them into the outreach tools.
Instantly launches cold email campaigns with warmup and webhook triggers on replies. We send 500K+ cold emails per month at ColdIQ across four ESPs simultaneously, across 80+ client accounts. 
Lemlist handles multichannel sequences across email, LinkedIn, and calls. 
LinkedIn Ads lets you programmatically build campaigns, sync audience lists, and retarget high-intent accounts.
Automation layer
For workflows that need to run continuously without prompting, n8n handles workflow orchestration for AI agents and API workflows. The skills repo includes a full n8n pillar covering workflow design, Clay integration, CRM automation, error handling, triggers, and self-hosting. Claude Code builds these workflows. n8n runs them.
System of record
Attio serves as our AI-native CRM with full API access and real-time webhooks. The skills repo references Attio in our signal sourcing and Clay CRM sync workflows.
Conversion layer
Cal.com handles scheduling infrastructure. Embed booking links in emails, auto-route meetings, and sync every booking back to your CRM.
Revenue layer
Dreamdata tracks every touchpoint from first signal to closed deal for attribution. 
Hyperline manages billing, subscriptions, usage metering, and invoicing. 
These are internal operations tools. They're part of our stack but not part of the skills repo.
 
What This System Actually Produces
 
This stack booked us 1,500+ meetings last year. We book 100+ qualified B2B meetings monthly. Here's what runs daily across our client base:
Full-cycle outbound 
A hiring signal fires in PredictLeads. Claude Code picks it up, enriches the contacts through the waterfall, scores them against the client's ICP criteria, assigns a tier, generates a personalized first line, and pushes the contact into the right outreach sequence. Zero manual steps between a signal appearing and a personalized email landing in someone's inbox.


 
Self-healing pipelines 
When a Prospeo lookup fails, Claude Code routes to FullEnrich. When an email bounces, it flags the contact and routes to a LinkedIn sequence instead. For workflows that need to run unattended, n8n handles the retry logic and fallback routing continuously.
Custom GTM agents 
Autonomous workflows that monitor signals, enrich contacts as they surface, route them to the right channel based on scoring, and report results back.
My Final Setup 
Conductor as the interface for running Claude Code and Codex. Claude Code as the central hub for all API and MCP calls. Vercel and GitHub for deployment. n8n for workflow orchestration when things need to run unattended.
Conductor is free and it's my go-to interface. The UI is much nicer than a terminal and it lets you toggle between Claude Code and Codex effortlessly.
Claude Code at $100/month is where everything happens. OpenAI Codex at $20/month I barely touch, honestly. Mostly for the occasional build that Opus can't crack. It plugs natively into Conductor, so maintaining access at $20/month is an easy call.
I started with Cursor at $60/month and loved the UI. Ran Claude Code inside it for months. Built some really sharp landing pages with it. But Conductor now covers everything I need with both Claude Code and Codex baked in, with an even slicker UI. I cancelled Cursor.
Lovable was useful early for prototyping and our first mini-tools. Solid starting point, but Claude Code is simply stronger for what we're doing now.
 
If you want access to the Claude Skills repo for GTM, repost this article and comment GTM below it. I'll send it your way.
